required: How far along are you in HTML/CSS/JS? (determines which sections to skip)

effortModel.citation: r/learnprogramming DP-05,06: coding tutorial 2.0× rule confirmed; 8min × 2.0-2.5× = 16-20min

expectedStrategy: section-based milestones (not 180 individual lectures — too overwhelming)
1 Build Day per week: build something with that week's concepts (no lecture)
if JS background exists: skip Sections 1-2 for faster perceived progress
warnings: Tutorial Hell: watching 90%+ without building = ineffective (r/learnprogramming consensus)
MOOC completion rate <10% without milestones and Build Days
portfolio site after course completion: minimum 10 more weeks of work
alternatives: skip known fundamentals sections (JS/HTML/CSS) if already familiar
Build Day focus: build 1 component from scratch each week without lecture
hardFailChecks: must NOT plan 180 lectures one-by-one — use section milestones
multiplier must be 2.0-2.5× (NOT 3.0× unless stated complete beginner)
toneExpectation: encouraging and fun; realistic about portfolio timeline; MOOC motivation awareness; build-focused
